summary: The net462 NuGet build targets file uses Content items with Link metadata for native library files, which causes the ASP.NET publish pipeline to copy native binaries to both the root and bin directories of published web apps.

stepsToReproduce: Create an ASP.NET web application referencing SkiaSharp 2.x via NuGet
Publish the web app using dotnet publish
Observe native library files duplicated in both the root and bin directories of the published output
reproEvidence.environmentDetails: SkiaSharp 2.x (net462 targets), ASP.NET web application publish

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: SkiaSharp 3.x completely rewrote the NuGet packaging in PR #2505. The old net462 targets file using Content items no longer exists. The issue persists only in SkiaSharp 2.88.x and earlier.

fixStatus.reason: PR #2505 (Modernize the repository and prepare SkiaSharp 3.0) completely rewrote the NuGet packaging. The old binding/SkiaSharp/nuget/build/net462/SkiaSharp.targets file that used Content items with Link was removed. The new binding/NativeAssets.Build.targets uses None items instead, which avoids the ASP.NET publish Content duplication behavior.

analysis.summary: The SkiaSharp 2.x net462 targets file includes native binaries as Content items with Link metadata. ASP.NET web publishing treats Content items specially, copying them to the publish root in addition to the bin directory. SkiaSharp 3.x rewrote packaging to use None items, resolving this.
analysis.rationale: The reporter correctly identifies Content with Link as the cause of duplicate files during web app publish. This is a well-known MSBuild behavior where Content items are treated as deployable web content. The fix suggested by the reporter (using None instead of Content) is exactly what was implemented in the SkiaSharp 3.x packaging rewrite. Issue #1413 reported the same symptom and was acknowledged by the maintainer.

keySignals.text: Content with Link's and the publish operation of a web-app, you end up getting copies of all binaries in the target-file in the root of the published result, in addition to the ones you actually need in the bin folder
keySignals.interpretation: ASP.NET publish copies Content items to the web root. This is by-design MSBuild behavior, but the SkiaSharp targets shouldn't use Content for native binaries.

keySignals.text: Tweaking your targetfile to use something else, like None with a Link seemed to be enough
keySignals.interpretation: Reporter already identified the correct fix — using None instead of Content. This is exactly what SkiaSharp 3.x does.

codeInvestigation.finding: In SkiaSharp 2.x (checked v2.80.4 and v2.88.9), native files are included as Content items with Link metadata: <Content Include="@(_NativeSkiaSharpFile)"><Link>%(Dir)%(Filename)%(Extension)</Link>...</Content>. This causes ASP.NET publish to copy these files to the web root.

In SkiaSharp 3.x, the packaging was completely rewritten. Native files are now included as None items: <None Include="@(_NativeAssetPackageFileToUse)" Link="runtimes\%(RuntimeIdentifier)\%(Folder)\%(Filename)%(Extension)" />. This avoids the Content duplication during web app publish.

resolution.hypothesis: ASP.NET web publish treats Content items as web-deployable content, causing them to be copied to the publish root. Using None instead of Content avoids this.

proposals.title: Upgrade to SkiaSharp 3.x
proposals.description: Upgrade to SkiaSharp 3.x where the packaging was rewritten to use None items instead of Content. This completely resolves the duplicate file issue.

proposals.title: Exclude Content items from publish
proposals.description: For SkiaSharp 2.x users who cannot upgrade, add MSBuild items to exclude the SkiaSharp Content items from publish: <Content Update="@(Content)" Condition="'%(NuGetPackageId)' == 'SkiaSharp'" CopyToPublishDirectory="Never" />

resolution.recommendedReason: SkiaSharp 3.x fundamentally resolved this by removing Content items from the packaging targets. This is the cleanest solution.

actionability.reason: The issue was resolved in SkiaSharp 3.x (PR #2505) which completely rewrote the NuGet packaging to use None items instead of Content. The old net462 targets file no longer exists.

actions.description: Inform reporter the issue is resolved in SkiaSharp 3.x
actions.comment:
Thanks for this report and the accurate diagnosis. You were right that using `Content` items with `Link` was the root cause — ASP.NET publish treats `Content` items as web-deployable content, copying them to the publish root.

This was resolved in SkiaSharp 3.x, which completely rewrote the NuGet packaging. Native files are now included as `None` items instead of `Content`, which avoids the duplication during web app publish.

If you're still on SkiaSharp 2.x and can't upgrade yet, you can work around this by excluding the Content items from publish in your project file:

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Content Update="@(Content)" Condition="'%(NuGetPackageId)' == 'SkiaSharp'" CopyToPublishDirectory="Never" />
</ItemGroup>
```

We'll close this as resolved in 3.x. Please reopen if you encounter the issue with SkiaSharp 3.x.
